ANNIDA HIDAYAH, I. S. N. A. (2023). THE IMPACT OF USING PODCAST AS A MEDIUM TO IMPROVE STUDENTS’SPEAKING SKILL (Quasi Experimental Research on the 10 th Graders of SMK SBI Grobogan in the Academic Year 2022/2023) (Doctoral dissertation, Universitas Islam Sultan Agung Semarang).

This is a doctoral dissertation titled "The Impact of Using Podcast as a Medium to Improve Students' Speaking Skill" conducted by Annida Hidayah in 2023, at Universitas Islam Sultan Agung Semarang. The study is a quasi-experimental research that aimed to evaluate the effectiveness of using podcasts to improve the speaking skills of 10th-grade students at SMK SBI Grobogan during the academic year 2022/2023.

what is instrument of study according to ANNIDA HIDAYAH, I. S. N. A. (2023). THE IMPACT OF USING PODCAST AS A MEDIUM TO IMPROVE STUDENTS’SPEAKING SKILL (Quasi Experimental Research on the 10 th Graders of SMK SBI Grobogan in the Academic Year 2022/2023) (Doctoral dissertation, Universitas Islam Sultan Agung Semarang).

According to the dissertation, the instruments used in this quasi-experimental research by Annida Hidayah to measure the impact of using podcasts on students' speaking skills were a pre-test and post-test speaking assessment, a questionnaire for the students, and a semi-structured interview guide for the English teacher. The pre-test and post-test speaking assessment consisted of six questions, whereas the questionnaire comprised 20 items on a five-point Likert scale. These items aimed to measure students' attitudes towards using podcasts as a medium to improve their speaking skills. Furthermore, the semi-structured interview guide was utilized to gather data from the English teacher's perspectives on the impact of using podcasts on students' speaking skill.
according to Burns, A. (1998). Teaching speaking. Annual Review of Applied Linguistics, 18, 102-123. What is speaking

According to Burns (1998), speaking refers to the productive skill of communicating information, thoughts, and feelings through verbal means. It involves constructing meaningful statements, using grammar and vocabulary appropriately, organizing ideas, and delivering a message through spoken language. In language teaching, speaking involves focusing on various aspects such as fluency, accuracy, pronunciation, and appropriacy to communicate effectively in different situations and contexts. Burns argues that speaking can be taught using a range of activities and techniques that emphasize interaction, negotiation of meaning, and practice of real-life communication.

regarding Pishkar, K., Moinzadeh, A., & Dabaghi, A. (2017). Modern English Drama and the Students' Fluency and Accuracy of Speaking. English Language Teaching, 10(8), 69-77. What is fluency in speaking

According to Pishkar, Moinzadeh, and Dabaghi (2017), fluency in speaking refers to the ability of a language learner to communicate effectively and smoothly using natural rhythm, appropriate pacing, and intonation. It involves producing speech effortlessly and with minimal hesitation while maintaining appropriate grammatical structures and vocabulary. Fluency is often associated with the ability to comprehend and produce language in a natural and spontaneous manner, without struggling to find the right words or phrases. In language teaching, fluency is considered an essential aspect of speaking skills, along with accuracy, which refers to the correctness of grammar, vocabulary, and pronunciation. Fluency and accuracy complement each other in language learning and are often targeted through a variety of classroom activities such as role-playing, storytelling, and debates, among others.
Derakhshan, A., Khalili, A. N., & Beheshti, F. (2016). Developing EFL learner’s speaking ability, accuracy and fluency. English Language and Literature Studies, 6(2), 177-186. use this to write more

The study conducted by Derakhshan, Khalili, and Beheshti (2016) aimed to investigate the effectiveness of implementing an intervention program tailored to develop EFL learners' speaking ability, accuracy, and fluency. The research involved a pretest-posttest design, where data were collected through a speaking test, accuracy test, and fluency test, which were administered before and after the intervention program. To ensure that the results were reliable, the researchers employed statistical analysis methods to analyze the data collected.

The study findings showed that the intervention program had a positive impact on developing the participants' speaking ability, accuracy, and fluency. The results indicated an increase in scores for both the speaking test and fluency test, with noticeable progress for most of the participants. In addition, the accuracy test scores also showed a significant increase, indicating the improvement of grammatical structures, syntax, and other factors related to language accuracy.

Based on the findings, the researchers suggested that a comprehensive intervention program that combines various activities and techniques aimed at developing learners' speaking ability, accuracy, and fluency is an effective way of promoting language learning for EFL learners. The study highlights the importance of providing language learners with ample opportunities to practice and interact with the language in a manner that focuses on both accuracy and fluency.

according to Derakhshan, A., Khalili, A. N., & Beheshti, F. (2016). Developing EFL learner’s speaking ability, accuracy and fluency. English Language and Literature Studies, 6(2), 177-186. What is speaking fluency

According to Derakhshan, Khalili, and Beheshti (2016), speaking fluency refers to the ability to communicate coherently and cohesively, using a range of linguistic resources, such as words, phrases, and sentences, without unnecessary hesitation or pauses. Fluency in speaking is a crucial aspect of language learning, as it determines how well a learner can express their ideas and thoughts in real-time communication. Fluent speakers can produce utterances smoothly, with appropriate intonation and natural rhythm, which makes communication more effective and engaging. Fluency in speaking is often linked with accuracy, as it involves the application of grammatical rules and vocabulary in real-time communication. Language learners can improve their speaking fluency through various activities, such as role-playing, group discussions, oral presentations, and debates, among others.

how to measure fluency according to De Jong, N. H., Steinel, M. P., Florijn, A., Schoonen, R., & Hulstijn, J. H. (2013). Linguistic skills and speaking fluency in a second language. Applied Psycholinguistics, 34(5), 893-916.

De Jong, Steinel, Florijn, Schoonen, and Hulstijn (2013) proposed a method to measure speaking fluency in a second language. According to the researchers, speaking fluency can be measured by calculating a range of speech rate measures, such as syllable production rate, articulation rate, and pause frequency. The researchers suggest that a combination of these measures is necessary for a complete picture of speaking fluency, as fluency involves a range of factors related to speech production.

Syllable production rate refers to the number of syllables produced per unit of time, and articulation rate refers to the number of phonemes or sounds produced per unit of time. These two measures offer an indication of the speed of speech production, with faster rates generally associated with greater fluency. In contrast, speakers who produce speech with long pauses may be perceived as less fluent.

To measure fluency, the researchers also suggest calculating the frequency and duration of pauses in speech. Increased pause frequency and longer pauses may be indicative of lower fluency levels.

Overall, De Jong, Steinel, Florijn, Schoonen, and Hulstijn (2013) suggest that using speech rate measures and pause frequency provide a more objective way of assessing speaking fluency across various speaking tasks. These measures can provide valuable insights into the factors that contribute to speaking fluency and can be used to identify areas for improvement in language teaching and learning.

De Jong, N. H., Groenhout, R., Schoonen, R., & Hulstijn, J. H. (2015). Second language fluency: Speaking style or proficiency? Correcting measures of second language fluency for first language behavior. Applied Psycholinguistics, 36(2), 223-243. according to this, how to measure speaking fluency

De Jong, Groenhout, Schoonen, and Hulstijn (2015) proposed a method to measure second language fluency that corrects for the influence of first language behavior. They argue that previous studies have not adequately considered first-language influences on second-language speech production, leading to inaccurate assessments of second language fluency. 

To measure second-language fluency accurately, the researchers suggest that two measures should be used: mean length of runs (MLRs) and frequency of pauses. MLRs refer to the number of syllables or words produced in a single speech production with no interruption or hesitations. The higher the MLR, the greater the fluency. Pauses are an indication of the degree of non-fluency. Frequent or lengthy pauses in speaking production are associated with non-fluent speech. 

To correct for first language influences on second-language fluency, the researchers suggest the use of an MLR ratio and pause ratio. The MLR ratio involves dividing the length of a speaking production in the second language by the maximum possible length of a single production in the speaker's first language. This allows researchers to correct for the influences of language-specific syntactic preferences. The pause ratio involves subtracting the mean pause duration in the first language from that of the second language, to correct for the influence of first-language pausing habits on second-language fluency measures.

Overall, the proposed method by De Jong, Groenhout, Schoonen, and Hulstijn (2015) aims to provide a more accurate assessment of second-language fluency by correcting for first language influences on speech production. They argue that this method helps to better distinguish measures of speaking proficiency from learned speaking behaviors, leading to more accurate evaluations of second-language fluency.
according to Bahrani, T., & Soltani, R. (2012). How to teach speaking skill. Journal of education and Practice, 3(2), 25-29. what is teaching speaking

According to Bahrani and Soltani (2012), teaching speaking involves the development of learners' ability to communicate effectively using spoken language. The aim of teaching speaking is to support learners in gaining proficiency in speaking tasks, such as initiating and maintaining conversations, presenting information and opinions, negotiating with others, and socializing in different contexts. Speaking skills are essential for everyday communication, and their mastery is a vital component of language learning.

Effective teaching of speaking requires the adoption of various teaching strategies, including integrating speaking activities into the curriculum, providing learners with authentic speaking tasks, using visual aids, offering feedback and correction, and fostering a positive learning environment. Moreover, Bahrani and Soltani suggest that teachers should use a communicative approach to teaching speaking, emphasizing learners' active participation in the learning process. This approach stresses the importance of interaction and negotiation of meaning between teachers, learners, and their peers.

Overall, the authors emphasize that teaching speaking is a complex process that involves integrating a variety of approaches to address learners' individual learning needs and preferences, and teachers should employ a range of appropriate teaching methods and strategies to support learners in developing their speaking proficiency and confidence.
according to Bailey, K. M., & Nunan, D. (2005). Practical English language teaching: speaking. What is the reason for teaching speaking

According to Bailey and Nunan (2005), there are several reasons for teaching speaking in English language teaching. Firstly, speaking is an essential element of communication, and its mastery is necessary for effective participation in social, academic, and professional settings. It is, therefore, crucial to develop learners' speaking skills to facilitate communication beyond the classroom. 

Secondly, speaking is a fundamental aspect of second language proficiency. Learners who can speak confidently and accurately are often perceived as having a high level of language proficiency. Additionally, speaking competence is necessary for successful performance on speaking assessment tests, such as those used for language proficiency evaluation or qualification exams.

Thirdly, teaching speaking provides learners with opportunities to develop intercultural competence. Communication often involves interaction with speakers from different cultural backgrounds, and the ability to adapt to cultural differences is essential for effective communication.

Finally, teaching speaking can also enhance learners' motivation and self-confidence. As learners gain competence and confidence in speaking, they become more motivated to engage in communication, which can lead to increased enjoyment and success in language learning.

Overall, the reason for teaching speaking in English language teaching is to develop learners' proficiency in communication, to support their performance in assessment, to cultivate intercultural competence, and to increase learner motivation and self-confidence.
what is podcast according to Rogers, C. V., & Medley Jr, F. W. (1988). Language with a purpose: Using authentic materials in the foreign language classroom. Foreign Language Annals, 21(5), 467-478.

Rogers and Medley Jr (1988) do not discuss the term "podcast" in their article. However, they define authentic materials as any type of text or media that reflects real communication in the target language or culture. Authentic materials may include newspaper articles, radio broadcasts, television programs, and films, among others. These materials provide learners with exposure to real-life language use and can help learners develop language skills beyond the classroom setting. Authentic materials promote learners' motivation to engage with the target language and culture, which is essential for language learning success. In this sense, podcasts, which are audio or video files made available online, can be considered authentic materials and used as educational resources in the foreign language classroom.

according to Alfa, R. R. (2020). Using podcast as authentic materials to develop studentsâ€™ speaking skill. JELLT (Journal of English Language and Language Teaching), 4(1), 65-74. what can podcast provide

According to Alfa (2020), podcasts can provide several benefits when used as authentic materials to develop students' speaking skills. Firstly, podcasts can offer learners exposure to real-life language use, providing them with examples of natural and authentic language in use. This exposure can help learners develop their listening and speaking skills in the target language. 

Secondly, podcasts offer learners various topics, accents, and speech rates, allowing them to develop their listening comprehension and understanding of different accents and dialects. Listening to different accents is important in developing learners' ability to comprehend and communicate with speakers of different backgrounds.

Thirdly, podcasts can provide learners with a language model to emulate. For example, learners can imitate the language used in podcasts to practice pronunciation, intonation, and rhythm. 

Fourthly, podcasts can also provide learners with the opportunity to develop their own fluency and accuracy in speaking. By listening to and practicing podcasts, learners can develop their speaking skills, such as the ability to produce coherent and well-structured sentences, and have better control over their use of grammar and vocabulary.

Lastly, podcasts are accessible and available online, providing learners with a flexible and convenient resource to improve their language skills outside the classroom. 

Overall, podcasts can provide learners with authentic language input, exposure to diverse accents and speech rates, language models to emulate, opportunities to develop fluency and accuracy, and a flexible and accessible resource for language learning.

Fitria, U., Vianty, M., & Petrus, I. (2015). USING PODCAST TO IMPROVE STUDENTSâ€™ LISTENING AND SPEAKING ACHIEVEMENTS. The Journal of English Literacy Education: The Teaching and Learning of English as a Foreign Language, 2(1), 55-68.  Sandoval Zapata, M. M. (2020). Podcast-based lessons: a useful tool to improve university students’ descriptive oral skills. Comunicación, 29(1), 52-68. according to the above studies, what are the effects of podcast on speaking fluency

The two studies provide some insight into the effects of podcasts on speaking fluency in language learning. 

Fitria, Vianty, and Petrus (2015) found that using podcasts as teaching materials can improve students' listening and speaking skills. The study involved a pre-test and post-test design, with a group of Indonesian students studying English language in an Indonesian university. The researchers found that using podcasts as part of classroom activities helped to improve students' speaking fluency, with the intervention group showing significant improvement in speaking accuracy, fluency, and vocabulary use compared to the control group.

Sandoval Zapata (2020) also found that podcast-based lessons can be a powerful tool to improve university students' descriptive oral skills. The study involved a group of Colombian university students, who received podcast-based lessons as part of their regular language learning curriculum. The researchers found that students who received podcast-based lessons improved their descriptive oral skills, in terms of fluency, coherence, and lexical richness.

Overall, the two studies suggest that using podcasts as teaching materials can be an effective way to improve students' speaking fluency in language learning. Podcasts offer learners authentic listening input, a source of fluency and accuracy models, and can provide opportunities for developing learners' speaking fluency through practice and feedback.
